The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs studied, the moons checked, the matches tested. It buys nothing. It only lifts you, station by station, from Wanderer to Blessed.

  • ✦Blessed by the Gods of Zodi100,000
  • ◉Awakened10,000
  • ·Wanderer0

Six stations in all — the full ladder lives on the Karmic Board.

狸 Cacomistle

0 of 13 chapters read

♊ Gemini and 猪 Year of the Pig

Cacomistle

You live one branch above where anyone thought to look, common enough to pass as rumor.

Zodi Animal · No. 036 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Cacomistle: Gemini's doubled voice crossed with the Pig's private appetite.

  • Gemini

    gives you two voices where most carry one, and the itch to use both before the night is over.

  • The Pig

    gives you an appetite that takes whatever the night actually offers, and no need for an audience while you take it.

  • The Cacomistle

    fuses both into one instinct: talk to the whole dark forest, and let almost no one actually see who is talking.

One strength, one cost: the same height that keeps you safe from the ground is the reason almost no one can find you when you actually want to be found. This reading traces where that instinct comes from, and what it becomes when it grows up.

Meet the Cacomistle

An animal common across two countries of forest, and still one of the hardest mammals in it to actually confirm.

A slender, cat-faced relative of the raccoon that spends its whole life in the cloud-forest and rainforest canopy of southern Mexico and Central America, one storey above the ground it almost never touches.

Everything this reading says about you is grounded in what the Cacomistle actually is: a genuinely common animal that stays almost entirely unconfirmed, a climber built to reverse direction completely without losing its grip, and a solitary forager that still calls out across the dark for hours at a stretch. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws its meaning from these real traits.

The distance was never the problem. Being the only one who kept an accurate count of yourself was always the point.

6 traits below

01of six

The uncounted resident

Animal fact

The IUCN Red List classifies the cacomistle as Least Concern, describing it as locally common across the northern part of its range, yet field surveys of its own forest routinely record it as one of the least-detected mammals present, cryptic behavior rather than true scarcity.

Our reading

You are the kind of present that never quite shows up on anyone's count. People who actually know you swear you are around constantly; people compiling the official record insist you are barely there. Both of them are describing the same forest correctly.

02of six

The missing floor

Animal fact

Nocturnal and highly arboreal, the cacomistle almost never comes down to the ground, moving through cloud-forest and rainforest canopy from sea level up to 2,000 metres.

Our reading

Your whole life happens one storey above where people are looking. It is not hiding, exactly. It is simply that the ground was never where you actually lived.

03of six

The reversible descent

Animal fact

Climbers in the cacomistle's own genus can rotate the hind foot nearly 180 degrees, letting the pads and claws keep their grip while the whole body descends a trunk headfirst.

Our reading

You can reverse a direction completely without losing your hold on anything. Most creatures back down carefully from what they climbed; you simply turn around and come down facing it.

04of six

The half-true name

Animal fact

The name cacomistle comes from the Nahuatl tlacomiztli, roughly "half mountain lion," a description of its cat-like agility despite belonging to the raccoon family and having no closer relation to any actual cat.

Our reading

You were named for what you resemble, not for what you are. People reach for the nearest familiar animal to explain you and end up with half of a description, which is usually as close as anyone gets.

05of six

The counterweight tail

Animal fact

The bushy, boldly ringed tail runs 390 to 530 millimetres, nearly as long as the 380 to 470 millimetre body, and works as a counterweight and balance aid through fast, high branches.

Our reading

Half of your visible length is the part that keeps the other half from falling. What people notice first, the flash of black and pale rings, is doing the quiet work of holding the rest of you steady.

06of six

The hours-long call

Animal fact

Solitary outside the mating season, cacomistles hold territories that can run past 130 hectares and exchange loud calls with neighboring individuals, sometimes for hours at a stretch, believed to mark the edges of that ground.

Our reading

You keep your own ground and still talk across it for hours, to someone you may never actually stand next to. Solitude, for you, has never meant silence.

The Cacomistle dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 036 / 144

☼ Cacomistle · Gemini × Pig

The Ringed Shadow

Common across the whole cloud-forest canopy, rarely confirmed, and calling the entire time.

Profile

Archetype
The Ringed Shadow
Central gift
Ranging wide, unconfirmed
Central shadow
Mistaking unseen for safe
Protective instinct
Climb higher, keep calling
Growth lesson
Let yourself be found
Power phase
New Moon
Element
Water
Ideal habitat
A high dark room with a voice that carries

Five traits carry this whole reading. See how many you recognize in yourself before you decide it fits.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    CacomistlePick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Gemini and the Pig meet

    Gemini brings

    • A second voice always ready, and the itch to use it the moment the night goes quiet
    • Quick, curious attention that catches the shift in a room before anyone names it
    • A comfort with being two things at once, visible and unreadable in the same breath

    The Pig brings

    • An appetite that takes whatever the night actually offers, not what was planned for
    • The patience to work a private territory alone and never make a performance of it
    • A trust that good ground, once honestly claimed, does not need to be defended loudly

    What the crossing makes

    Fused well, you are the one who moves through a room, a job, a whole shared life constantly, and is still somehow never quite where people expect to find you. You talk easily, forage widely, and hold your own ground without needing anyone to watch you do it. People who know you describe someone quite different from the person compiling the official account, and you are both of them at once.

    Where they pull against each other

    Gemini wants to be heard; the Pig would rather not be watched while it works. So the same voice that carries for hours across the dark canopy is aimed at no one in particular, and the ground you hold so well stays a rumor to everyone but the few who already know where to listen. The whole reading is about telling apart a distance you are keeping on purpose and one you have simply never gotten around to closing.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Gemini
    Pig
    50 Gemini In balance Pig 50

    In balance: you range widely and still keep one true territory, talking when it matters and staying unseen the rest of the time on purpose rather than by default. This is the Cacomistle at rest.

    Your element

    水

    Your element is Water

    Water's archetype: The Sage · in the Cacomistle: The Canopy Current

    Water's archetype in this system is the Sage. In the Cacomistle it rises into something more specific: a current that runs through the high canopy instead of along the ground, felt everywhere in its territory and confirmed by almost no one who goes looking for it. Water at its best moves toward what it needs and finds the level meant for it: the current that takes the route no one else thought to check, the flow that reaches without needing to be traced back to its source. In excess it goes to vapor, present everywhere and pinned down nowhere, until no one can get an accurate read on where it actually is. The Shadow chapter wears this same signature.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal: for the Pig, that is Water, not from Gemini's Sun sign directly. Your Western sign adds a second note here, but not a Wu Xing one: Gemini's own element is Air, which sits outside the five-phase system entirely (Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water only), so it colors the reading without changing the arithmetic. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionNorth
    SeasonWinter
    VirtueWisdom
    ColorBlack

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    金Metal feeds Water, as the exact branch feeds the current

    In the generating cycle, Metal is the source that lets Water flow. For a person built like the Cacomistle, this is the one precise decision, the exact branch, the exact person, that turns wide, constant range into something that actually lands. Sharpen the aim, and the current stops evaporating into pure motion.

    Balances you

    土Earth contains Water, as the den holds the whole range

    In the controlling cycle, Earth checks Water, keeping it from spreading past its bounds. For you, Earth is the one fixed den, the one appointment you actually keep, that stops your territory from dissolving into constant, unaccounted motion. A little Earth is what keeps the whole range legible, at least to you.

    You generate

    木Water feeds Wood, as the current feeds the fig tree

    In the generating cycle, Water is the source that lets Wood grow. What you range out and gather across a whole night becomes the thing that feeds someone else, the call that reaches exactly the person who needed to hear it. When the current is received and not only sent, it turns into real growth for whoever was actually listening.

    You restrain

    火Water checks Fire, as the dark canopy meets the noise

    In the controlling cycle, Water governs Fire, capable of putting it out entirely. For you, this is the cooling effect you have on a room running too loud, too urgent, too determined to get you pinned down immediately. You can talk a demand back down to something reasonable, though pushed too far the same instinct smothers urgency that only wanted a straight answer.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    Your element here comes from one thing alone: the fixed element of your year-animal, the Pig's Water. It is a true starting note, but it is only that, one phase carried by an animal that spends most of its life one level above the ground it is nominally rooted to.

    Bazi, the Four Pillars, reads the full elemental weather of your birth: year, month, day, and hour, each with its own stem and branch. It shows whether your Water runs as a wide current or a narrow one, and which of the five phases your life is actually short on, Metal especially, given how much of this crossing runs on motion without a fixed edge.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Water in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Water finds its closest match in the Sacral chakra, Svadhisthana. It governs adaptability, resourcefulness, and the making of a secure base, and its image for you is the single high den you always return to after ranging an entire territory of canopy in the dark. Its bija sound is VAM; its element there is water.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Sacral chakraSvadhisthanaBija: VAMElement: water
    ChakraVisit your Sacral chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    水ElementThe Water element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    You climb before you're counted

    The moment a room starts taking attendance, you are already one branch higher, still talking.

    Before you have decided anything, your body has. Someone asks a direct question, a situation calls for you to stand still and be seen, and you are already moving up and sideways, out of the exact spot where anyone could point at you. Not fleeing, exactly, foraging, the way a cacomistle works a fig tree it has already half finished.

    It reads as busyness from the outside. Inside it is an animal that learned early that the ground is where things get decided about you instead of by you. You keep talking the whole time, which is usually enough to convince a room you never actually left.

    02 · Capacity

    You cover more ground than anyone accounts for

    The people who actually know you get the full range: the voice, the appetite, the territory held quietly and well.

    Grown up, the same instinct becomes real range. You take in more of a room, a job, a whole shared life than the official count ever credits you with, and you do it without needing the record corrected. The voice that carries for hours is the same one that makes people feel accompanied even when you are three branches away and out of sight.

    This is the Cacomistle at its best: present everywhere it matters, confirmed nowhere it doesn't, and entirely at ease with the gap between the two. You can be constantly in motion and completely reliable in the same breath.

    03 · Defense

    The height is protecting the actual count

    Everything you climb away from is in service of staying a rumor to anyone who has not earned the confirmed sighting.

    The distance is not the point. The point is what gets decided about you once someone thinks they have you counted, catalogued, placed. You learned early that being fully seen means being fully assessed, so the climbing, the wide foraging, the calls sent out instead of conversations held close, all of it exists to keep the real tally of you somewhere only you can check.

    It has worked. That is the trouble with it. The people compiling the record have genuinely never gotten a fix on you, and neither, some nights, have the people who love you and are still listening for where the voice went.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the voice that keeps its own count

    You answer real closeness with the same climb you use on a threat, then call it just being how you are.

    What trips it. Someone gets close enough to actually confirm you, ask a direct question, expect a straight answer, want to know where you really stand.

    What your mind says. If they get an accurate count, they get to decide something about me. Better to stay the rumor a little longer.

    What you do. You go up a level. You talk more, not less, filling the air with motion and voice until the actual question gets lost in it.

    What it costs. The people trying to reach you learn to settle for the calls instead of the animal, an entire bond carried on <b>through a voice in the dark canopy that never quite comes down to be seen</b>.

    In love

    You are constant and present in every way that doesn't require standing still long enough to be fully known.

    At work

    You cover more ground than anyone else on the team and still manage to be the hardest person to actually pin down for a straight answer.

    With friends

    People describe you completely differently depending on which branch they last caught you on.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still range wide, and you still talk more than you're seen. You just let a few people get the confirmed sighting.

    Nothing about you goes still in the wrong way. You keep the range, the appetite for a whole night's worth of forest, the voice that can carry for hours without tiring. What changes is the reflex. You stop treating an accurate count as a threat and start handing it out on purpose, to the few people who have shown they can hold it without trying to cage the rest of you in it.

    The climbing stays. It just stops being the automatic answer to being asked a direct question, so the animal behind the calls finally gets to be more than a rumor to the people who actually love it.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I answer the call from…

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Cacomistle

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Cacomistle you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Gemini Pig crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Cacomistle, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best when you can range widely and still keep one territory nobody else is auditing.

    01 / 05

    You lead by covering the ground no one assigned you

    You lead from motion, not from the front of the room. Your team feels the effect of your range before they can describe what you actually did: gaps get filled, problems get scouted three steps ahead, and somehow you were never in the one meeting where credit got handed out.

    The risk is that you cover so much ground so quietly that no one learns to read your actual position, and the team ends up guessing at your workload instead of asking. Wide, unconfirmed coverage eventually reads as absence, even when it is the opposite.

    02 / 05

    A wide range, and no one keeping score

    You do your best work with real territory to range across and no one standing at the edge of it with a clipboard. Give you the whole night's worth of forest and the freedom to work it your own route, and you outlast almost anyone.

    What drains you is a fixed desk, a single tree, constant visible check-ins. Boxed into one confirmed spot, the cacomistle does not slow down quietly. It goes looking for the nearest higher branch and a reason to be there instead.

    • A genuinely wide territory
    • Freedom from constant check-ins
    • A few people who track you honestly
    • Credit that doesn't require your presence to claim it

    03 / 05

    Wide sampling, slow commitment

    You decide the way you forage: sampling several branches before settling on one, which reads as indecision when it is actually thoroughness. The correction is naming the deadline out loud, so the circling has an edge to land on.

    You delegate the visible tasks easily and keep the actual territory-holding to yourself, because handing over ground feels like losing the one thing that is unambiguously yours. The reverse is the practice: let someone else hold a piece of it, and notice it is still there when you check.

    04 / 05

    Where you stall

    The loud stall is scattering across too many branches at once, so much range that nothing gets a full pass and the whole territory reads as thin.

    The quiet stall is disappearing into the coverage itself: so much motion, so many calls sent out, that you lose track of whether anyone is actually receiving them, and the work becomes its own kind of hiding.

    05 / 05

    The long game

    What compounds for you is range that becomes reputation: the people who have tracked your actual coverage over years know exactly how much ground you cover, even when the official record undercounts it.

    The practice is small and physical: once a week, stay on one branch long enough for someone to actually find you there, and let them see the work instead of just hearing about it.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    A high dark room with a voice that carries

    The cacomistle forages across a wide territory of canopy each night and returns to a den it rarely if ever brings to ground level. Translated to a room, that means height, dark, and range: a private base up and away from the main floor, with a clear line for the voice to travel out from it. Each recommendation below follows from that: give the range somewhere to return to, and it will.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Water is yours; Metal is what feeds it. For the Cacomistle that means deep cloud-forest charcoal-green for the canopy you actually live in, warmed by the pale, banded cream and umber of the tail everyone actually notices.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundWaterAbout 45% of the room

    Canopy dark

    The color of the cloud forest at the hour you are most yourself. It is the base note of your element and it reads to you, before you've thought about why, as cover with room to move.

    SupportWaterAbout 29% of the room

    Understory shade

    A near-black green for the corners, the color of the forest floor you almost never actually touch. It grounds the room without pinning you to it.

    AccentMetalAbout 17% of the room

    Ring-tail umber

    Metal feeds Water in the five-phase cycle. The pale, banded warmth of your own tail is the source that keeps the dark from going stagnant.

    SparkMetalAbout 9% of the room

    Fig-flesh pale

    One warm, ripe note, the color of the fruit that pulls you across the whole territory in a single night.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    Set it up high if you can, a loft, an upper floor, a desk with a real view down. The cacomistle does not work from ground level, and neither, most nights, do you.

    Bedroom

    This is the den in the canopy. Keep it dark, elevated in feeling if not in fact, and genuinely private. You recover somewhere above the traffic, not in the middle of it.

    Entry

    Give the doorway a sense of ascent, even a single step up, a plant that draws the eye higher. Arriving home should feel like climbing back to your actual level.

    Living area

    Furnish it for range, not for a fixed seating chart. A few good spots you actually move between suit you better than one designated chair.

    The calling corner

    Keep one spot, a window seat, a balcony, a good chair near the charger, built for reaching people who are not in the room. The forager in you rests easier when the line out is always open.

    Reflection

    Hold a small, dark, elevated corner for the new-moon practice of choosing who gets the confirmed sighting this cycle.

    Elements to avoid

    • Ground-floor rooms with no sense of height or vantage
    • A home that is all common area with nowhere private and elevated to retreat to
    • Silence built into the space, with no way to reach out from it when you need to
    • Décor that performs a stillness you don't actually practice
    The five-minute reset

    A five-minute range reset: get physically higher, a stairwell landing, a balcony, even standing on a stool, put one call out to one real person, and let your eyes adjust fully to low light before deciding what happens next.

    水 Feng Shui
    for the
    Cacomistle

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Water element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Water element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the Ringed Shadow

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      The range was built to keep you safe from being fully counted, not to keep you a rumor forever. The work is not to stop climbing. It is to choose, on purpose, who gets the confirmed sighting, and then to actually hold still long enough for them to get it.

    • A ritual

      On the new moon, when the sky offers the most cover, name one person you will let confirm you this cycle. Say it out loud, once, and then actually be findable to them.

    • In your space

      Keep one object at your highest, darkest spot that marks a person who has already gotten the real sighting, so the room reminds you the range already has an exception.

    • In one bond

      This week, tell one person exactly where you actually are, instead of letting the calls stand in for the visit.

    • At work

      Let one person track your actual coverage for a week, on purpose, instead of correcting the record yourself after the fact.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The cacomistle runs on the darkest turn of the month, the phase that gives full cover to a creature already built to move unconfirmed. It fits an animal whose whole life happens above where anyone thought to look: this is the time to range fully and to choose, on purpose, one person who gets the confirmed sighting. Use it to move and to be found by exception, not by accident. Avoid using it to vanish completely; the point is full range with one deliberate exception, not total disappearance.

    Your next new moon · Power Moon

    Tuesday, July 14, 2026

    4 days from today.

    When it arrives, go out under full cover and run your widest circuit of the month. Then name one person, and actually let them find you on the branch you land on.

    1. Mon, Jul 13Start clearing your actual coordinates. Tell one person, plainly, where you really are this week.
    2. Tue, Jul 14 · New MoonRange fully under full cover. Name the person who gets the confirmed sighting this cycle, out loud.
    3. Wed, Jul 15Stay on one branch a little longer than usual. Let the person you named actually find you there.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    人rén怕pà出chū名míng，豬zhū怕pà壯zhuàng

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally people fear becoming famous, pigs fear becoming fattened

    Meaning Standing out invites danger: fame exposes a person the way a visibly fattened pig invites the knife.

    The cacomistle has never needed telling. It stays lean on the official record on purpose, common across two countries of forest and still filed as a rumor, because a full, confirmed count has never once worked in a small animal's favor. The proverb treats this as a rule to fear. You have simply always known it, and built a whole life a canopy above where the count gets taken.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Pig. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Cacomistle loves

    You will stay in constant contact with someone for years before you let them get an accurate fix on where you actually are.

    Care, for you, looks like a voice that reaches, not a body that stays put, constant contact traded for confirmed presence. Underneath it is a quiet rule: if I keep moving and keep talking, no one gets close enough to pin an accurate account of me to anything.

    How you show love

    By staying in constant contact. You call, check in, and range back toward someone all night if that is what the bond needs, and you rarely ask them to come find you instead.

    What makes you feel safe

    A partner who takes the voice as seriously as the visit, and does not demand you land on one branch before you are ready.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your distance reads as detachment. It is usually just the shape your presence naturally takes.

    Your conflict pattern

    You keep talking and keep moving rather than stopping to be pinned down by the actual disagreement.

    What to practice

    Say it plainly: I am still here, I am just three branches over. Come find me instead of waiting.

    How you show up

    As the friend who is somehow always in touch and rarely in the room, checking in from wherever the night has taken you.

    What they may misunderstand

    That you are unreliable because you are hard to pin to a plan. You are actually one of the most consistent people they know, just not in a way the calendar can see.

    What to practice

    Commit to one fixed plan this month and actually show up to the branch you named.

    How you show up

    As the one who is always somewhere in the family's range, rarely in the room everyone else is sitting in.

    What they may misunderstand

    That your absence from the gathering means absence from the family. You are closer than the seating chart suggests.

    What to practice

    Say out loud where you actually are this season, instead of letting people guess at your coverage.

    How you show up

    As the one who covers more ground than the job description accounts for, and rarely files the paperwork proving it.

    Your conflict pattern

    You avoid the direct confrontation and instead simply relocate to a different part of the problem.

    What to practice

    Bring the disagreement to one fixed meeting instead of handling it in passing, from three branches away.

    Good support for a Cacomistle does not require a fixed location to trust the bond is real. It takes the calls as seriously as the visits, does not treat wide range as evasion, and stays close enough that when you do land on one branch for the night, someone is actually there to notice.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    木

    Wolverine

    ♈ Aries × 虎 Tiger

    1950, 1962, 1974, 1986, 1998, 2010, 2022

    Secret friendsYour Water feeds their Wood

    The Tiger is the Pig's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair. Your Water feeds their Wood: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Wolverine

    Match II · of three

    木

    Fennec Fox

    ♊ Gemini × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Same trineYour Water feeds their Wood

    The Rabbit shares the Pig's trine, so effort between you compounds. Your Water feeds their Wood: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Fennec Fox

    Match III · of three

    水

    Golden Snub-nosed Monkey

    ♌ Leo × 猪 Pig

    1947, 1959, 1971, 1983, 1995, 2007, 2019

    Same yearShared Water

    Two Pig years run on the same clock. Two Water natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Golden Snub-nosed Monkey

    One branch higher

    Where to go from the Cacomistle

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Gemini changes across the other eleven years

    Reading Gemini through a different year-animal shows how much of this crossing is the doubled voice, and how much is the Pig's particular way of holding private, unwatched ground.

    All Gemini animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Fellow creatures who are far more present than the record admits, guarded by shell, height, or a plumage the forest keeps mistaking for nothing at all.

    Coconut CrabCancer · Year of the Rat Moon BearCancer · Year of the Tiger Golden PheasantLeo · Year of the Rabbit

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Wide-open, constantly witnessed natures, herd-bound and surface-dwelling, the full visibility you find hardest to sustain.

    Asian ElephantCancer · Year of the Ox Sea OtterCancer · Year of the Rabbit Humpback WhaleCancer · Year of the Dragon

    Same year, other skies

    The Pig's private appetite and quiet diligence, worn under eleven other suns.

    Honey BadgerAries · Year of the Pig BongoTaurus · Year of the Pig CapybaraCancer · Year of the Pig Golden Snub-nosed MonkeyLeo · Year of the Pig
    Explore all of these crossings
    Star-nosed MoleRoseate SpoonbillDeep Sea AnglerfishTree KangarooGiant ArmadilloPlatypusBlue Glaucus

    Same sky, other years

    Gemini's doubled voice and quick attention, wearing eleven other bodies.

    MeerkatGemini · Year of the Rat Three-wattled BellbirdGemini · Year of the Ox Bat-eared FoxGemini · Year of the Tiger Fennec FoxGemini · Year of the Rabbit
    Explore all of these crossings
    Superb LyrebirdCuttlefishSwiftRock WrenKeaFork-tailed DrongoAfrican Grey Parrot

    Step back and see the system

    The hubs this crossing hangs from.

    All Gemini animalsThe Western wing All Pig animalsThe Eastern wing The Water elementShared ground The full MenagerieAll 144 animals

    The last chapter

    The Cacomistle is yours.
    Now find out who actually gets the confirmed sighting.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Cacomistle.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the new moon, your power phase, and range the whole territory under full cover. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Ringed Shadow · Gemini × Pig · Water 水

    Your line to keep

    I am the Cacomistle: common across the whole cloud forest, rarely confirmed, and still talking the entire time.

    狸

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Cacomistle now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Cacomistle wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Metal is what feeds this nature; Earth is what holds it in place.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Gemini Pig shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Cacomistle before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Cacomistle · 036 / 144 · Water 水

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Cacomistle until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Cacomistle, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Cacomistle Test

    Do you range the way the Cacomistle does?

    The Cacomistle is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Cacomistle, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Cacomistle You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the Ringed Shadow, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Bassariscus sumichrasti
      • Animal Diversity Web · Bassariscus sumichrasti
      • Animal Diversity Web · Bassariscus astutus
      • NCBI PMC · Diversity and Abundance of Arboreal Mammals in a Tropical Rainforest in Southeast Mexico

      Last reviewed 2026-07-09

      The Primal Zodiac, 144 animals

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Find your Zodi Animal ✦

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.
